title: alyssum lappaceum
intro: Alyssum lappaceum, also known as Madwort, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Alyssum lappaceum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
